title: achillea borealis var. fusca
intro: Achillea borealis var. fusca, also known as Yarrow or Bear's Foot, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Yarrow include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving circulation, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic and a mild laxative. Yarrow can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and to support the immune system.
